title: Configuring the Uniface Debugger
text: Configuring the Uniface Debugger You can configure the Uniface Debugger by setting breakpoints and watches, and selecting preferences and settings from the View menu. The Uniface Debugger settings, watches, and breakpoints make up its state, which can be saved to the configuration file for repeated use. Choose View > Settings , click the desired tab, and select the various diagnostic settings you want. Then click Apply . Tab Purpose I/O Print Choose the level of I/O messages that are displayed in the message frame or transcript window. Tracing Choose the level or type of ProcScript-related tracing to be displayed in the message frame or transcript window. Triggers Choose the triggers you want to be enabled or disabled for debugging, or those that you want to be set as breakpoints. Errors Set a specific ProcScript error or exception value ( $procerror ) as a breakpoint. See Configuring the Debugger Error and Exception behavior . Message Frame Specify whether the clrmess ProcScript instruction is enabled or disabled (the default in the Debugger), and display location of application messages. For information on specific settings, click F1 to display the online help. Note: The Triggers and Errors tabs enable you to set general breakpoints. You can also set specific breakpoints in the Debugger main window. See Working with breakpoints . Choose View >Preferences , and select the desired Debugger startup, close, and display preferences. By default, On close: save state & preferences is selected. This ensures that the current state is automatically saved when the Debugger is closed. You can change this, and any other preferences as desired. Optionally, save the current state as a named state. Click Add and provide a name and description for the state. Click the Save to insert a new section with this name and description, containing the current active state, in the configuration file. If you want to update the default state using the current active state, click Update Default . Tip: This is handy for establishing a minimum set of preferences, settings, breakpoints, and watches, that you can use for any debugging session. Note: If you have set any watches or breakpoints, these are also saved under that named state. Click Save to save the current settings and preferences. Related Links Configuring the Debugger Error and Exception behavior The following procedure allows developers to enable or disable Uniface debugger breakpoints based on the defined errors or exceptions break-on conditions on the Settings > Error configuration tab. Debugger State The state of the Debugger consists of current settings, watches, and breakpoints. The current state can be saved as a named configuration for repeated use. Was this topic helpful?
